description: Courgettes are incredibly productive and satisfying to grow. A single plant can produce dozens of fruits throughout the summer. They love the warmth and will thrive in a large pot on a sunny balcony.

characteristics.waterNeeds: High — requires daily watering in summer
characteristics.soilType: Rich, well-drained potting compost with added manure

care.sowIndoors: Sow seeds 1.5cm deep on their sides in 7.5cm pots. Keep at 20°C on a warm windowsill. Seeds sprout in 7–10 days.
care.sowOutdoors: Sow directly in large pots from late May. Sow 2 seeds 1.5cm deep and keep the strongest seedling.
care.hardenOff: Gradually acclimatise to the outdoors over 7–10 days in late May. Move outside during the day, bring in at night.
care.plantOut: Move to a final large container (at least 45cm wide) after the last frost (mid-May in London).
care.watering: Water daily in hot weather. Keep the soil consistently moist but avoid wetting the leaves to prevent mildew.
care.feeding: Once the first flowers appear, feed weekly with a high-potash liquid fertiliser (tomato feed).
care.harvest: Pick regularly when fruits are 10–15cm long to encourage continuous production.
care.overwintering: Annual plant — pull up and compost after the first frost in autumn.

propagation.seedDescription: Large, flat, cream-coloured oval seeds. Very easy to handle. Viable for 3–5 years.

deadheading.notes: Harvesting the fruit effectively 'deadheads' the plant. You can also harvest the male flowers for cooking.

seedSaving.method: Let one fruit grow into a large marrow. Leave it on the plant until the skin is hard and the vine dies back. Scoop out and dry seeds.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Seeds are contained within the fruit itself.

commonProblems: Powdery Mildew — white dusty coating on leaves. Improve airflow and water at base.
Slugs and Snails — protect young seedlings after planting out.
Poor Pollination — if fruits shrivel, try hand-pollination.
funFact: If you don't harvest courgettes, they can grow into giant marrows over a metre long in just a few weeks!
